idea-plugin.name: Codex Assistant
idea-plugin.description:
Run Codex CLI from inside JetBrains IDE via the built-in terminal.

How to Open

  1. Shortcut: Press Ctrl+Shift+X or option+command+X to open the terminal in your current project.
  2. Toolbar: Click the icon in the top toolbar.

Note: If a session is already running, you cannot open a new one.

Sending References (Send File Path To Codex Terminal)

  1. Code: Highlight code and press Ctrl+Alt+X (or use the right-click menu) to send the file path and line numbers.
  2. Full Files: If no text is selected, the current file path is sent.
  3. Files/Folders: Right-click an item in the Project View and select "Copy to Codex Terminal" to send the path.
  4. Multiple Items: You can select several items to send their paths separated by spaces.

Settings

  1. Location: Go to Settings → Tools → Codex Assistant.
  2. Codex Executable Path: The default setting is "codex." If the file is installed in a location not included in your system PATH, you should enter the full absolute path here.
  3. Additional CLI Arguments: This field is empty by default but allows you to add specific flags to the "codex" command for every execution.
  4. Request Confirmation Before Code Changes: This is enabled by default. It automatically adds a security flag that requires your approval before any untrusted code modifications are made.

action.description: Open Codex in the built-in terminal with the current file
Copy the selected code to the running Codex terminal
